question: What is the sensor used for collecting the data?
keywords: sensor device instrument equipment camera microphone accelerometer calibration hardware
summary: Describes a sensor used for collecting the data.
description:
Describes a sensor that was used for collecting the data
and its calibration value as a key-value pair.

retrieval.hypothetical_passage:
## Data Collection

Driving footage was captured using a forward-facing **FLIR Blackfly S** RGB camera (1920x1200 at 30 fps) and a **Velodyne VLP-16** 3D LiDAR rotating at 10 Hz, both mounted on a roof rack and calibrated against a Novatel SPAN-CPT IMU/GNSS unit. Medical scans were acquired on a **Siemens Magnetom Skyra 3T MRI** (T1- and T2-weighted sequences, 1mm isotropic voxels) and a **GE Revolution CT** scanner; ultrasound recordings used a Philips EPIQ 7 with a C5-1 curved-array probe at 5 MHz. Audio was recorded via a Sennheiser MKH-416 shotgun microphone sampled at 48 kHz. All sensors were intrinsically calibrated; extrinsics refined via ICP where applicable.

extraction.instruction: Extract every sensor used to capture the data, together with any reported calibration values, sampling rates, model numbers, or mounting configuration.
extraction.field_spec: SPDX property `dataset_sensor`. List of key-value pairs (`sensorName: calibrationOrSpec`). Format: 'Velodyne VLP-16: 10 Hz; FLIR Blackfly S: 30 fps 1920x1200; Novatel SPAN-CPT IMU: 100 Hz'.
extraction.output_guidance: Capture sensor make/model where stated, then any calibration value (rate, resolution, intrinsic/extrinsic notes). Separate multiple sensors with semicolons. If only the sensor type is named with no calibration, include the type with value `unspecified`. Return 'Not found.' if no sensors are mentioned; do not infer sensors from dataset modality (e.g. an image dataset is not necessarily camera-sourced if it is synthetic or scraped).
